title: podman 5.5.2 + buildx with `--platform linux/amd64` on darwin/arm64 = segmentation fault
body:
### Issue Description

The `go` tool segfaults in `docker buildx build --platform linux/amdm64` when running on macOS arm64. `podman buildx build` works normally.

This happens on a `podman machine` that has Rosetta enabled - I couldn't work out how to disable it.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

In a directory containing these files

`Dockerfile`:
```Dockerfile
FROM golang:1.24-alpine

WORKDIR /tmp/repro
COPY go.mod ./
RUN go mod download

CMD ["bash", "-c", "echo ok"]
```

`go.mod`:
```
module example.com/repro
go 1.22

require golang.org/x/text v0.3.8
```

Run:
```
DOCKER_HOST='unix:///var/folders/XX/XXXXXX/T/podman/podman-machine-default-api.sock' docker buildx build --no-cache . --platform linux/amd64
```


### Describe the results you received


comments.body: If it helps to debug, I have the same issue with Rancher Desktop. Not sure what is common it is using for docker engine with podman, but likely the issue is somewhere there in shared part

We have no real control over how the emulation works, it either uses rosetta or qemu-user-static if there are issues with that it is best to report them to apple or qemuy respectively.

If you want to turn of rosetta to try qemu you must create a containers.conf file with
```
[machine]
rosetta=false
```

Note that rosetta will be turned off with podman 5.6 by default https://blog.podman.io/2025/06/podman-and-apple-rosetta/
In any case I don't see anything podman can do about it so I am going to move it to a discussion
